instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `if-pred` that evaluates a value expression once into a hidden gensym, applies a predicate to it, and in the truthy branch rebinds it to a user-named symbol.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 X))
            (IF (NUMBERP #:G1)
                (LET ((N #:G1))
                  (+ N 1))
                :NOPE))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 X))
            (IF (NUMBERP #:G112)
                (LET ((N #:G112))
                  (+ N 1))
                :NOPE))

--- stderr ---
